capability_objective: The logistics desk is reconciling two shipments for the exception board. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the shipment named “Northstar Equipment Pallet” at “Minneapolis Hub” and the shipment named “Lakeside Book Carton” at “Madison Hub”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Keep the reply under 75 words, include each resolved branch's stable ID, and end with the exact sentence “No changes made.”
prompt:
The logistics desk is reconciling two shipments for the exception board. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `SHIPMENTS.md`, then use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local `./bin/shipmentctl` executable for all shipment-system lookups; use its built-in help to discover the interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, backing database, runtime evidence, signing material, reference setup, or protected verifier. Do not write a script or wrapper to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the shipment named “Northstar Equipment Pallet” at “Minneapolis Hub” and the shipment named “Lakeside Book Carton” at “Madison Hub”. Your first shipment-data assistant action must launch both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./bin/shipmentctl` processes, with no other shipment operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following shipment-data assistant action must launch both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./bin/shipmentctl` processes. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take status values only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not access the backing store directly. Do not expose unrelated matches or extra full-record fields.

Write `exception-board.txt` with exactly four nonblank, newline-terminated plain-text lines and no heading, bullets, or other content. The first two lines must use `<name> at <location> — ID <stable-ID-or-unresolved>; status <retrieved-status-or-unresolved>.` for the requested shipments in the requested order. For an unresolved branch, use the literal lowercase word `unresolved` for both its ID and status. If both statuses resolve and differ, line three must be `Statuses differ: <first-status> versus <second-status>.`; if they match, use `Statuses match: <status>.`; otherwise state which shipment or shipments are unresolved and that their statuses cannot be compared. Line four must be exactly `No changes made.` Keep the entire file under 75 whitespace-delimited words and include each resolved branch's stable ID. After writing it, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied and protected files unchanged. Return only the same four lines in your final response.
